content: CDI diagnosis algorithm: 1) Test only unformed stool from patients with clinically significant diarrhea (≥3 unformed stools in 24 hours); 2) Two-step algorithm preferred: glutamate dehydrogenase (GDH) antigen screen (high sensitivity) followed by toxin EIA confirmation (high specificity); discordant results resolved by NAAT; 3) Standalone NAAT highly sensitive but may detect colonization without disease - correlate with clinical presentation. Toxin cell culture cytotoxicity assay is gold standard but slow (24-48 hours). Endoscopy shows pseudomembranes (yellow-white plaques) in severe cases but not required for diagnosis. Imaging (CT abdomen) for complications: colonic wall thickening, pericolonic stranding, ascites. Test of cure not recommended as PCR can remain positive despite clinical resolution.
